item.title: Carney, Starmer meet amid deepening Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer met in London to discuss the escalating Middle East war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The leaders condemned Iran’s attacks, warned of mounting civilian and economic costs, and stressed the need to reopen the critical oil shipping route.

item.title: A Toronto councillor wants to create public grocery stores to tackle rising food costs. Would that work here?
item.description: The pilot would have four city-run grocery stores across the city, prioritizing neighbourhoods with limited access to grocery stores and lower…

item.title: Strong winds, rain and possibly snow expected during March break in Toronto
Nathan Phillips Square skaters can finally come in from the cold — but thanks to whom?
Toronto police union says officers rights were violated in ‘offensive overreaction’ fuelled by Project South scandal
What the Frank Stronach trial is telling us about how Ontario police investigate historical sex assault allegations
Poilievre arrives late to the auto debate with a plan from 1965
item.description: It’s as if the Conservative Leader can’t believe the happy days are gone and thinks a simple plan will bring them back

item.title: Ontario urges federal government to legalize pepper spray for self-defence
Doug Ford wants jets at Billy Bishop. Here’s what is at stake for Toronto’s waterfront — and why some say there’s a ‘missing ingredient’
Doug Ford said ‘whisper jets’ were coming to Billy Bishop. Here’s what aviation experts say about airplane noise
item.description: Ontario's premier said the jets coming to Billy Bishop airport would be quieter than the current turboprop planes.

item.title: Poilievre pitches tariff-free auto industry pact with U.S.
item.description: Conservative Leader says Canada can’t steer away from its biggest export market if it wants to revive sector

item.title: Company offers use of private deep-water Baffin Island port to Department of National Defence
item.description: Defence Minister was non-committal when asked about the possibility of the military using Milne Port

item.title: Doug Ford to seek an injunction to block Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto
item.description: The premier's move comes in the wake of a similar move by two city councillors.

item.title: Canadian competitive sports shooters caught in the crosshairs of sweeping federal gun control reforms
item.description: Sports shooters including practical shooting athletes and Olympic pistol shooting disciplines say the sustainability of their sport has been u…

item.title: Poilievre unveils auto plan aiming for tariff-free access to U.S. market
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre unveiled the party's most substantial auto strategy to date under his leadership — one he says will both bring back production to Canada and be "highly attractive" to partners south of the border.

item.title: Canada, Nordic leaders aim to deepen ties as trade being used as ‘coercive tool’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is concluding his time in Norway meeting with that country’s prime minister, along with the leaders of Denmark, Iceland, Sweden and Finland.

item.title: Thousands attend pro-Palestinian rally in Toronto after judge denies Ford government’s injunction to stop it
Conservative leader says his plan is ‘the only hope’ for Canada’s auto industry
Mark Carney helping push Liberals ahead of the Conservatives, poll suggests
Nordic leaders meet in Oslo with Carney as Arctic tensions sharpen over Greenland, Russia
item.description: Leaders from the five Nordic countries and Canada gathered in Oslo for a mini-summit on Arctic security amid rising geopolitical tensions. Discussions follow a massive NATO exercise in northern Norway and focus on Russian military risks, the fallout from U.S. threats to seize Greenland and energy security.

item.title: Carney says Canada a reliable oil exporter with moves to increase production
Prime Minister’s office declines to weigh in on Order of Canada petition for Don Cherry
item.description: Conservative MP gathering signatures for online petition to award Canada’s highest honorary distinction to hockey commentator

item.title: Carney defends his economic record after one year in office
item.description: Prime Minister is in Norway as part of ongoing efforts to open new trade and business opportunities

item.title: Canada’s Olympic medal letdown masks the real sports crisis in this country. One potential game changer is already on the table
item.description: Ottawa's Future of Sport Commission has a recommendation to bridge the gap between Olympic-level sports and the grassroots.

item.title: Carney, Norway PM push message of oil-market stability as war in Middle East rattles global supply
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and Norway’s Jonas Gahr Støre stressed energy stability during talks in Oslo as the Middle East war rattles global oil markets. Canada will release 23.6 million barrels following an International Energy Agency call for coordinated action. Both leaders also urged de-escalation and pledged closer Canada-Norway cooperation on Arctic security.

item.title: Carney government in talks with Saskatchewan to fast-track energy projects
Michael Ford reveals why he may run for mayor of Toronto — and what he’s done since leaving Queen’s Park
item.description: The nephew of Premier Doug Ford says he has been consulting the public for months about a potential mayoral run.

item.title: Why Canada is helping Canadians flee the Middle East — and what it costs
‘One elbow up, one down’: The Carney government after its first year
Federal government planning to revamp funding for Canadian athletes, says Carney
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says Ottawa is planning to revamp its funding for Canadian athletes and will examine the issue "very deliberately" over the next six months — news that comes just before a federal commission on the future of Canadian sport releases its final report.

item.title: New café offers taste of Quebec independence
item.description: When you walk into the Club Pays coffee shop in Montreal, the first thing you see are the posters with Quebec sovereignty slogans plastered on every wall.

item.title: He’s useful in a crisis – but is he any good at politics? A look at Mark Carney’s first year
item.description: For the first time, Canada has a Prime Minister figuring out how to be a politician on the job, in front of everyone

item.title: Canadians might be soon waiting longer to have their air travel complaints heard
item.description: The federal government is currently weighing whether to renew funding meant to address a massive backlog of airline passenger complaints — funding that if not extended could result in Canadians having to wait longer to have their cases heard.

item.title: Former NATO chief says he doesn't think allies will be pulled further into Middle East conflict
item.description: The former secretary general of NATO says he doesn't think allies will be pulled further into the conflict in the Middle East, but he's concerned the ongoing war with Iran will benefit Russia's economy and take the world’s attention away from the invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Parks Canada planning to cull dozens of deer on historic Quebec island
item.description: Parks Canada is proposing to kill dozens of white-tailed deer at a storied historic site near Quebec City, saying the deer population on the island presents a threat to rare plants and the safety of employees flying in. However, an animal-rights advocate is calling for the agency to use non-lethal solutions to deal with the deer.

item.title: Quebec Conservatives sour on Don Cherry nomination as party pushes Order of Canada petition
item.description: Opposition is mounting among Quebec's federal Conservatives to their own party's push to award the Order of Canada to controversial hockey commentator Don Cherry.

item.title: Federal departments, agencies release details on plans to find billions in internal savings
item.description: Reductions include lower than planned spending for a construction project at Transport Canada and scaling back legal support for veterans looking to appeal rulings about their benefits

item.title: Ford government to end funding for remaining supervised consumption sites: advocates
item.description: Ontario’s remaining supervised consumption sites will be forced to close after Premier Doug Ford’s government informed them Friday that the province is planning to pull their funding, harm reduction advocates say.

item.title: Ottawa to allow rural employers to increase proportion of temporary foreign workers
item.description: Ottawa will allow rural employers to have a greater proportion of low-wage temporary foreign workers on their payroll on a time-limited basis, as part of new measures the federal government announced on Friday.

item.title: Mark Carney rejects Trump administration move to ease sanctions on Russian oil
Premier and cabinet ministers will be exempt from FOI requests under new legislation
item.description: The Ontario government says it will be introducing legislation to keep secret documents from the premier’s office as well as the offices of his cabinet ministers.

item.title: Politics Insider: Carney rebukes U.S. for easing sanctions on Russian oil sales
item.description: Ontario to introduce legislation exempting the Premier, cabinet ministers from FOI requests

item.title: Ottawa to extend temporary work permits in Quebec for up to 12 months
Doug Ford is changing a law to shield politicians from scrutiny
U.S. begins forced-labour investigation into 60 countries, including Canada
item.description: Offending nations could face tariffs of as much as 25%, analysts say

item.title: Toronto Al-Quds Day rally to go as planned despite Ford’s call for injunction: lawyer
As Trump pauses Russian shadow fleet sanctions, Carney says Canada holding firm
item.description: Three world leaders — two of them members of the G7 — stood united in opposition Friday to the Trump administration's decision to ease sanctions on Russia's shadow fleet of oil tankers.

item.title: Unions, MPs warn funding cuts could affect services for veterans
Questions linger about duties of incoming national security and intelligence adviser
item.description: As Canada's national security and intelligence adviser prepares to leave her post, there are questions about the exact role her successor will play.

item.title: IN PHOTOS | Highlights from NATO's Cold Response exercise
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store were near Bardufoss, Norway, to watch NATO's Cold Response. The joint military exercises, which Norway hosts every two years, involves 32,000 troops, 100 aircraft and 30 warships from 14 nations.

item.title: Ontario to give retailers the option of opening on Family Day and Victoria Day
item.description: TORONTO - Stores across Ontario could soon open on Family Day and Victoria Day under a new bill the provincial government plans to introduce.

item.title: Conservatives nominate candidate in Scarborough byelection
item.description: The seat was left vacant after former Liberal cabinet minister Bill Blair resigned to take over as Canada's high commissioner in the U.K.

item.title: Carney says Canada's job creation still way ahead of U.S. despite losing 84,000 jobs last month
item.description: The Canadian economy lost more jobs in February than it has in a single month in the last four years — but Prime Minister Mark Carney says when compared to the United States, we're doing pretty good.

item.title: Canada rejects Iraqi government’s claim that Ottawa will take detainees from ISIL era
Environment committee engaging in ‘junior high behaviour’: former climate advisers
How John Tory and Michael Ford have shaken up Toronto mayor’s race, according to a new poll
Doug Ford says Ontario will expropriate Billy Bishop airport to bring jets in
Ottawa’s ‘failure’ to disclose attack on airbase in Kuwait with Canadian forces ‘shameful,’ Tory MP says
item.description: Canada has an operational support hub at the airbase, which also hosts the United States Air Force
